author: muhsinider
text: How to convert sympy.core.numbers.Float types to just native python float type

author: muhsinider
text:
my code finds the roots of a nonlinear equation as follows:İ

T(K)= 399  
-0.0937999028234458 - 4.15858122699766e-24\*I <class 'sympy.core.add.Add'>  
T(K)= 449  
0.00365302829594929 <class 'sympy.core.numbers.Float'>  
T(K)= 499  
0.0688176963782414 <class 'sympy.core.numbers.Float'>  
T(K)= 549  
0.137782216780566 <class 'sympy.core.numbers.Float'>  
T(K)= 599  
0.202639297806409 <class 'sympy.core.numbers.Float'>  
These results can not be used for any other python calculations.  
How can I convert them to normal float numbers or complex numbers? It works if the data was converted to a list by hand but not directly within code

author: oscarbenjamin
text: Just call `float` or `complex` on the numbers.

author: oscarbenjamin
text: GitHub issues are not for asking for help with using sympy. You should use the sympy mailing list or StackOverflow for that.
